meta.title: Lights ON!!
meta.description: Turn on a red LED using a digital pin.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding basic LED circuitry and digital pin output
meta.code_hint: Set the digital pin to HIGH to illuminate the LED

walkthrough.instruction: Put the LED on the breadboard with the long leg in row 12, column E and the short leg in row 11, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 11, column D and the other leg in row 7, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the LED doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the Arduino's GND pin to the breadboard at row 7, column E.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino Pin 8 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal that turns the LED on and off.

meta.title: Lights ON!!
meta.description: Turn on a red LED using a digital pin.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding basic LED circuitry and digital pin output
meta.code_hint: Set the digital pin to HIGH to illuminate the LED

walkthrough.instruction: Put the red LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) in row 12, column E and the short leg (cathode) in row 11, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — it's called the anode!

walkthrough.instruction: Put the 220Ω resistor on the breadboard with one leg in row 11, column D and the other leg in row 7, column D.
walkthrough.tip: The resistor limits current so the component doesn't burn out.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D8 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 7, column E to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
